title: End of Evangelion good guys bad guys
display_title: End of Evangelion good guys bad guys
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion good guys bad guys? Post by Zeak » Sat Feb 02, 2008 4:36 pm Just came to my attention that in the end of evangelion who are the good guys and who are the badguys. Ill explain a little. Nerv's role was to defeat the angles and prevent 3rd impact. Seele wanted it Gendo wanted it The Jssdf where understood that nerv wanted to start 3rd impact. So tecknicly there the good guys in a way. So are the proportion of nerv that are trying to stop it. but because there under Gendos command that sort of makes Nerv the badguys at the end. Bloody seele lol

[]: Post by Xard » Sat Feb 02, 2008 5:14 pm There's no good guys and bad guys in Evangelion. Angels aren't bad guys. NERV aren't bad guys. JSSDF aren't bad guys. Even SEELE aren't bad guys (though complete looneys and quasireligious ruthless fanatics) There even isn't heroes in the series for the matter. As far as Right and Wrong goes (diferent from Good vs. Bad) there's only one aspect in whole show that has clear stand over it: Shinji's rejection of Instrumentality was Right thing. However not even that can be described as "good" or "bad" thing.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sat Feb 02, 2008 5:18 pm The real "villain" in EoE is Keel Lawrenz, though he's still doing what he believes is right.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 02, 2008 5:38 pm The villain of the piece is human nature.

[]: Post by universalperson » Sat Feb 02, 2008 5:51 pm The villian in EOE was Anno. Everyone knows that.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:15 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The villain of the piece is human nature.

[]: Post by OtakuCrave » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:31 pm All great theories so far, but I just wanted to add in my own two cents. I believe Shinji is the main antagonist in the film. I believe this could be considered a valid reasons due to these situations: 1. He doesn't want to fight when he has the opportunity too, thus furthering the destruction of Nerv; death, mayhem, and destruction senselessly ensues. 2. He denies the sustainability of third impact; everything he fought for is deliberately lost by his own hands. 3. In a way, he is somewhat responsible for Misato's death due to him being uncooperative in their escape to Eva-01. What do you people thinkl?

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:39 pm No. There are reasons for all of that which fit squarely under human nature. There was no stoping the violence anyways, SEELE saw to that which makes them the secondary villians. Since their characters were never fleshed out they don't fit under the human nature theme.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:43 pm OtakuCrave wrote: What do you people think?

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:44 pm No we don't cause Reichu said stop.

[]: Post by OtakuCrave » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:49 pm Thanks for the thread redirection Mr. Tines. @Anonymous_Evafan: Well, human nature as the main culprit is very subjective to me when the one in control would eventually be Shinji. Are you saying that human nature is entirely submissive to war when it is necessary? SEELE wanted to take the peaceful route at first, attempting to hack the Magi system. It is only when this is denied do they use force. So saying you "couldn't" stop the violence from occurring is ruled out in my opinion.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:51 pm OtakuCrave wrote: Thanks for the thread redirection Mr. Tines.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Feb 02, 2008 7:22 pm OtakuCrave wrote: Well, human nature as the main culprit is very subjective to me when the one in control would eventually be Shinji.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 02, 2008 8:38 pm I agree with Mr. Tines, and universalperson. Also, if this becomes some kind of Shinji / Seele / EoE / Inevitability etc. etc. thread, I am so gonna lock it. This thread also feels strangely familiar, but I don't feel like looking for a previous incarnation right now.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Feb 02, 2008 9:26 pm Reichu wrote: This thread also feels strangely familiar, but I don't feel like looking for a previous incarnation right now.

[]: Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Feb 02, 2008 9:27 pm There's one on the "bad guys" here Who exactly are the bad guys in Evangelion?" -Another one I've found.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 02, 2008 10:12 pm I'll play with the merge tool later if another mod doesn't beat me to it.
